item.title: Dad advice. It’s been a really tough week to be a dad as one (adult) kid has broken up with their long time SO. We are all sad. Here’s some “fatherly advice and how to’s” from my stash I send to my sons. Upvote and comment with your own if you like.

item.dc:creator: DarthPutinKGB

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1171 (Rose petals)
item.description:
Also, why is this coffee I made with water with this river over here so delicious? Who are you? Ahhhh. Bliss. Let's get this bread.
*sips raki and coffee watching flame war in the comments as the comments section balkanize*
On a whim, i added dried rose petals to my coffee, which is a self-made blend of about 75% light roast Ethiopian Yirgchaffe and 25 percent Guatamalan medium roast.
Pippin, 24/7. (Actually, can someone more talented than me photoshop Pippin's head on the dude above on this meme?)
I am trying.
I'd get a happy meal for this
This and the isle at church for the Easter Procession on May 5th* are the only isles I see myself walking down anytime in the next year (*Eastern Orthodox, remember?)
Me, this whole week, especially after Urgent Care decided after wasting three hours of my life NOT to give me a physical because I've been prescribed Lithium.
It would be nice. But I finally have an appointment with an actual doctor tomorrow at 10. Which means I lose four tours and associated tips that this year typically would come to around $80-per tour
Oh,and the dishwasher STILL has not been fixed yet. Qwmmrrrearrrgh.
Barring this, if I had decendents, I'd ask my decendents to make me into a skull cup.
Me on Mondays, actually, but yeah
As alternative life choices go, if more waters existed in Arizoma, this might be how I'd go.
Awwww, fine.
Oh, FYROM.
Me then... and now.
Mood.
"Well shit."
Hahahahaha
..... fuck. Also this has BIG @kirbyvictorious book vibes...
I haven't been kissed or made out for almost a year.
I should have used Lavender instead of the rose petals. The flavor isn't terribly strong.
Pippin Tax! Today's message from Pippin is: "Okay Pippin. Whewwww. Good boy. Hahahahhaha. Good boy, Pippin. Pippin."
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Wednesday wholesome dump to make you smile, my dudes :)
item.description:
1. The way the mom laughs
2. Can you guess the theme?
3. This doggooo
4
5. A fan gives Johnny Depp a plush toy and security tries to take it
6. Refer to this post if you are having a shitty day
7
8. Man, even If I get submitted by Bane, I'm still bragging to everyone
9. Finally
10
11
12. Did it work
13. Just friends enjoying the sun
14
It's never to late to be creative. What are you passionate about? For me it's game dev and I'm currently working on silly "Stanley Parable"-inspired game with my brother. I basically learned Unreal Engine from scratch just so I can make games like the ones that made me happy as a kid. If you like to help us please wishlist the game on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. Have a great rest of the week. Don't forget to hydrate and be mindful of negative thoughts.

item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: Did a thing! 12 months, zero dollars given to the power company
item.description: Solar + Batteries = Freedom from coal, gas, and the power company. Edit: this blew up way more than I expected, thanks everyone. To answer a few common questions: Yes we still pay a basic connection fee, but we export enough excess solar to offset the cost. No this is not currently a cost savings for us, but the economics of it are incredibly complicated. You'd need to consider current and future cost of electricity from the grid, how long the panels/battery will be in service for, inflation, interest on the loan, tax benefits, etc...
item.dc:creator: TheWhiteBarry
